name: Parque Regional El Valle y Carrascoy — Running circular to Pico del Águila
name_original: Parque Regional El Valle y Carrascoy — Running circular hasta el Pico del Águila
region_intro: Jogging in Carrascoy Y El Valle offers diverse landscapes within the Cordillera Sur mountain range, characterized by rugged and varied topography. The region features extensive Aleppo pine forests and Mediterranean scrub, providing a fragrant and visually engaging environment. Runners will encounter steep slopes, wadis, and ravines, contributing to the dramatic scenery. Key elevations include the Sierra de Carrascoy and the distinctive "Cresta del Gallo," offering panoramic views. Best jogging routes in Carrascoy Y El Valle The most popular jogging route is Trail run circular to the top of Relojero, a 7.5 miles (12.0 km) trail that takes 1 hour 42 minutes to complete. This difficult route ascends to the Pico del Relojero, offering expansive views of the park. Another top favourite among local runners is Parque Regional El Valle y Carrascoy — Running circular to Pico del Águila, a difficult 6.7 miles (10.8 km) path. This route provides a challenging run through varied terrain, including pine forests and rocky outcrops. Local runners also love the Mirador Barranco del Sordo loop from Los Garres, a 5.3 miles (8.6 km) trail leading through the natural landscape, often completed in about 56 minutes. Jogging in Carrascoy Y El Valle is defined by pine-covered mountains, rugged topography, and Mediterranean scrub. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging ascents. The routes in Carrascoy Y El Valle are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 5 reviews. More than 700 runners have used komoot to explore Carrascoy Y El Valle's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Carrascoy Y El Valle?
A: Carrascoy Y El Valle offers a wide selection of running routes, with &lt;strong&gt;over 45 trails&lt;/strong&gt; available. These routes cater to various fitness levels, from moderate paths to more challenging ascents.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of running trails in Carrascoy Y El Valle?
A: The running trails in Carrascoy Y El Valle present a varied challenge. While there is &lt;strong&gt;1 easy route&lt;/strong&gt;, the majority are more demanding, with &lt;strong&gt;11 moderate routes&lt;/strong&gt; and &lt;strong&gt;37 difficult routes&lt;/strong&gt;. This ensures options for both casual runners and those seeking a significant physical challenge.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the running routes in Carrascoy Y El Valle?
A: You can expect a diverse and rugged terrain in Carrascoy Y El Valle. The routes traverse extensive Aleppo pine forests and Mediterranean scrub, offering a fragrant and visually engaging environment. Runners will encounter steep slopes, wadis, and ravines, with key elevations like the Sierra de Carrascoy and the distinctive "Cresta del Gallo" providing dramatic scenery and panoramic views.

Q: Are there any easy running routes for beginners in Carrascoy Y El Valle?
A: Yes, there are options for beginners. While most routes are moderate to difficult, there is &lt;strong&gt;1 easy route&lt;/strong&gt; available. For a moderate option that's still accessible, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38250218" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador Barranco del Sordo loop from Los Garres&lt;/a&gt;, which is 5.3 miles (8.6 km) long and takes about 56 minutes to complete.

Q: Can I find circular running routes in Carrascoy Y El Valle?
A: Absolutely. Many of the running routes in Carrascoy Y El Valle are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. A popular example is the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e900710305/trail-run-circular-to-the-top-of-relojero" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trail run circular to the top of Relojero&lt;/a&gt;, which offers expansive views from Pico del Relojero.

Q: What natural features or viewpoints can I see while running in Carrascoy Y El Valle?
A: Carrascoy Y El Valle is rich in natural beauty and offers several stunning viewpoints. You can encounter features like the &lt;strong&gt;Cresta del Gallo&lt;/strong&gt;, known for its sharp rocky edges and sweeping panoramas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1562562" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Relojero Peak&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2857853" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico del Águila&lt;/a&gt; also provide breathtaking vistas. The park itself, often called the "green lung" of Murcia, is characterized by extensive pine forests and diverse Mediterranean flora.

Q: Are there any specific landmarks or attractions along the running paths?
A: Yes, several landmarks and attractions enhance the running experience. You might pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1562577" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Base of the Cresta del Gallo&lt;/a&gt;, a notable mountain pass. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5134334" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View from Castillo de la Asomada&lt;/a&gt; offers historical context and panoramic views. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5390619" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Valle Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; itself is a significant natural monument to explore.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Carrascoy Y El Valle?
A: The running trails in Carrascoy Y El Valle are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of &lt;strong&gt;4.7 stars from over 5 reviews&lt;/strong&gt;. More than 700 runners have explored the varied terrain, often praising the peaceful atmosphere, the scent of resin from the pine forests, and the stunning panoramic views, especially from peaks like Relojero.

Q: Are there any long-distance running trails in Carrascoy Y El Valle?
A: Yes, for those looking for longer runs, Carrascoy Y El Valle has several options. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e900710305/trail-run-circular-to-the-top-of-relojero" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trail run circular to the top of Relojero&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 7.5 miles (12.0 km) route. Another excellent choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40374719" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Relojero loop from Baños y Mendigo&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 7.1 miles (11.4 km) with significant elevation.

Q: Is Carrascoy Y El Valle suitable for family runs?
A: Carrascoy Y El Valle offers a range of trails, and while many are challenging, there are options suitable for families. The diverse landscapes and natural features provide an engaging environment. For a family-friendly experience, look for the easier or moderate routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38250218" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador Barranco del Sordo loop from Los Garres&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 5.3 miles (8.6 km) path.

Q: Are there any running routes that offer views of the Cresta del Gallo?
A: Yes, the distinctive "Cresta del Gallo" is a prominent feature in the park, offering sharp rocky edges and sweeping panoramas. Many routes in the area provide views of this unique landscape. While not a specific route, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1562577" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Base of the Cresta del Gallo&lt;/a&gt; is a highlight you might encounter or view from nearby trails, offering an almost lunar landscape.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Carrascoy Y El Valle?
A: Carrascoy Y El Valle is generally suitable for running throughout much of the year due to its Mediterranean climate. However, spring and autumn offer the most pleasant temperatures, making them ideal for exploring the trails. During summer, it's advisable to run early in the morning or late in the afternoon to avoid the midday heat.
tips: One of the many peaks within the El Valle and Carrascoy Regional Park. From its 623 meters above sea level you can appreciate very nice views of the entire park. You will have a demanding but picturesque trail going up and you can take one of the variants to descend if you prefer. Depending on the path you choose, there will be more loose stone, so be careful.
